decisions.reasoning: Iran war Day 8+, Pentagon $100B+ war bill. FT reports US weapons makers order backlog at record. Interceptor/precision munitions depletion rate faster than expected - restocking orders needed. Defense spending is fiscal policy driven, FOMC rate hikes do not affect. NOC already raised guidance confirming structural demand.

Retatrutide (triple-G) passed two Phase III trials, filing planned for 2027. Obesity franchise moat widening - next growth driver in $100B+ market. Defensive pharma is natural hedge in risk-off macro. STAT+ has questions on heart benefit data but overall thesis intact.

Record Q4 orders + $60bn backlog + gross margin surprise. SpaceX order confirms AI server demand is structural. Even as tech sector sold off on GOOGL/TSLA, SMCI surged +19% - market is differentiating real AI winners. Momentum + fundamentals aligned.

Oil broke $100 - real supply disruption (Hormuz+Houthis hitting Saudi tankers+Kazakhstan Black Sea pipeline), not just risk premium. AAL already cut guidance on fuel costs - real economy transmission confirmed. FOMC rate hike risk is secondary, supply disruption dominates. Upstream energy stocks have real earnings upside.

First major airline to significantly cut 2026 earnings outlook due to fuel costs at $100 oil. Fuel is largest variable cost for airlines. AAL is canary in the coal mine - if DAL/UAL follow with downgrades, entire transportation sector gets revalued. This is not isolated story but oil-to-macro contagion real-economy transmission evidence. If oil sustains >$95, more transport/consumer discretionary stocks will face pressure.

Q2 complete miss: margin continued declining + FCF turned negative, $214B market cap wipeout. Not overreaction, structural margin compression. EV price war + discount strategy is race to the bottom. Robotaxi pilots (Orlando/Tampa) are long-dated call option not near-term revenue driver - revenue still zero. Musk "spend as fast as we can" comment triggered panic. Analysts cutting price targets across the board. $180-200 before considering.

Bull: $4,180 macro trendline bounce, geopolitical safe haven demand (Iran conflict), oil-driven inflation fear boosting haven demand. Bear: Gold failed to rally on Iran war (fell from $4,000+ to $3,680) - extremely bearish signal. Warsh hawkish Fed keeps real yields elevated, zero-yield asset extremely disadvantaged in real yield >2% environment. If gold cannot rally on war, something structural is shifting.

Bull: Q2 fastest revenue growth in 15 years, AI data centre turnaround story gaining traction. Market extremely undervalued INTC - contrarian value play. Bear: Seeking Alpha downgrade "A Great Quarter Doesnt Make It A Good Investment" - one quarter does not equal trend change. Silicon photonics interesting long-term but years away. Wait for post-earnings pullback before entry.

Bull: Vera CPU strategy expansion, Jensen $4T bet, TSM confirms multi-year AI demand, China cheap AI models may actually boost chip demand (Jevons paradox). Bear: AI capex reckoning narrative repricing high-multiple growth, $207 key level rejected twice, Moonshot AI bypassed export ban getting chips triggering stricter export control risk. FOMC rate hikes heavily impact ultimate high-beta AI play.
